changes_made: Changed k8s_image_latest_tag_avoidance to k8s_image_latest_tag_prohibited for consistency with existing naming patterns
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the concept of patch management within Kubernetes. The naming conventions mostly follow the prescribed pattern, but one function name was adjusted for consistency with existing patterns. The functions address key areas of patch management: node OS, control plane components, and etcd. The image function was slightly off in naming but technically accurate in intent.
naming_improvements: Use 'prohibited' instead of 'avoidance' for image tag checks to align with existing conventions
title: Patch Management
step1_reasoning: Patch management in Kubernetes can be mapped to ensuring that the underlying node operating systems are up-to-date, control plane components are patched, and images used in the cluster avoid using the 'latest' tag to ensure version control. Additionally, etcd, as a critical component of Kubernetes, should be patched regularly.
